meta_title: Impact Doors in Melrose Park, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs HVHZ-rated impact doors in Melrose Park, FL. ESW & CWI brands, 170mph protection, insurance savings. Licensed & insured. Get a free quote today!
h1: Impact Doors in Melrose Park, FL — HVHZ-Rated Protection for Your Home
intro:
Melrose Park sits quietly in the heart of Broward County, just a few miles west of Fort Lauderdale's bustling coastline, but don't let its inland location give you a false sense of security. With a 170 mph wind zone designation and full High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) status, homes throughout the 33312 ZIP code face some of the most demanding weather conditions in the entire United States. For homeowners in Melrose Park, upgrading to certified impact doors isn't just a smart home improvement decision — it's a critical layer of protection for your family, your property, and your financial investment.

The neighborhood surrounding Melrose Park Elementary is a tight-knit, established community where many homes have stood for decades. In fact, approximately 57% of homes here were built before 1994 — before Florida's post-Hurricane Andrew building code reforms took effect. That means the vast majority of entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors in the area were never designed to meet today's rigorous HVHZ standards. With average home values hovering around $300,000, protecting that asset with properly rated, code-compliant impact doors is one of the most responsible decisions a Melrose Park homeowner can make.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've spent years helping South Florida families just like yours secure their homes against the unpredictable fury of Atlantic hurricane season. Our team understands the specific structural needs and permit requirements that apply to Melrose Park properties, and we work directly with the City of Fort Lauderdale Building Division to ensure every installation is fully permitted and inspected. Whether you're replacing a worn front entry door, upgrading aging sliding glass doors at the back of your home, or adding elegant French doors to your living space, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings the craftsmanship, local knowledge, and brand-name products your home deserves.

section_why.h2: Why Melrose Park Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
section_why.body:
When it comes to impact door installation in Melrose Park, experience and accountability matter more than price alone. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings over 60 years of combined industry experience to every project, and unlike many competitors who rely on subcontractors, every single installation we perform is done entirely in-house. That means the same licensed, insured professionals who quote your project are the ones who show up on installation day — no middlemen, no surprises, and no shortcuts on code compliance.

Our team has completed more than 500 homes across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, which means we're intimately familiar with the specific requirements that apply to HVHZ properties in Melrose Park. We know the City of Fort Lauderdale Building Division's permitting process inside and out, and we handle all paperwork on your behalf. From the initial product selection and measurement phase to final inspection sign-off, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire process so you don't have to navigate bureaucratic red tape on your own.

We carry an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau and have earned the trust of hundreds of Broward County homeowners through transparent pricing, respectful communication, and installations that stand up to real-world scrutiny. Our commitment to quality doesn't stop at aesthetics — we install impact doors that are specifically tested and certified for the HVHZ, ensuring your home meets or exceeds every standard required by the Florida Building Code. For a neighborhood where pre-1994 construction is the norm rather than the exception, that level of expertise is invaluable.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing also understands the financial realities of Melrose Park homeowners. We offer flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down, making it genuinely accessible to protect your home without draining your savings account. We believe that world-class hurricane protection shouldn't be reserved for luxury properties — every family in South Florida deserves a safe, secure home.

section_history.h2: Melrose Park's History With Hurricanes Makes Impact Doors a Necessity, Not a Luxury
section_history.body:
Melrose Park and the surrounding Broward County communities have weathered at least 10 named storms since 1990, and the damage patterns left behind tell a consistent story: doors and windows are among the first points of failure during a major hurricane. When Hurricane Andrew devastated South Florida in 1992, it exposed catastrophic weaknesses in residential construction that were especially pronounced in older housing stock — exactly the kind of pre-1994 homes that make up more than half of Melrose Park's residential landscape. Andrew's destruction prompted Florida to overhaul its building codes, but homes already standing were left in place, still carrying the vulnerabilities of a less demanding era.

Hurricane Wilma struck in October 2005 and reminded Broward County residents that even a storm tracking well to the south or west could deliver devastating wind loads to inland communities like Melrose Park. Wilma produced sustained winds and powerful gusts that ripped through entry doors, shattered sliding glass panels, and allowed catastrophic water intrusion into homes that had never been upgraded. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017, which tracked directly up the Florida peninsula and subjected the entire county to prolonged tropical-storm and hurricane-force winds — a slow-moving punishment that tested every door, frame, and seal along the way.

The lesson from each of these storms is clear: a door that wasn't designed for HVHZ conditions is a liability waiting to become a catastrophe. Once a door or entry point fails during a hurricane, internal pressurization can escalate rapidly, leading to roof uplift, wall failure, and total structural compromise. Impact doors from Premier Impact Windows & Roofing eliminate that domino effect by maintaining the building envelope under the most extreme wind and debris conditions South Florida can produce.

section_code.h2: HVHZ Compliance and Broward County Building Code Requirements for Impact Doors
section_code.body:
Melrose Park's location within Broward County places it squarely inside Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone — a designation shared only with Miami-Dade County and representing the most stringent residential building standards in the entire nation. Under the Florida Building Code's HVHZ provisions, all impact doors installed in the 33312 area must carry product approval through the Florida Department of Business and Professional Regulation (DBPR) and must be tested to withstand the specific wind pressures and large missile impact criteria defined for this zone. A standard impact door rated for other parts of Florida may not be sufficient here — HVHZ demands a higher bar.

For Melrose Park specifically, permits for impact door replacements are pulled through the City of Fort Lauderdale Building Division, which enforces HVHZ compliance rigorously. Every permitted door replacement requires a Notice of Commencement, product approval documentation, and a final inspection to confirm proper installation of the frame, anchoring, and weather sealing. Attempting to install impact doors without permits in this jurisdiction is not only illegal — it can void your homeowner's insurance policy and create serious complications at the time of sale or during a future insurance claim.

With a 170 mph design wind speed rating required for the Melrose Park area, every ESW and CWI impact door we install is selected and certified to meet or exceed that threshold. Our team submits all required product approval documentation and handles the permitting process from start to finish, ensuring your installation is fully compliant, inspected, and on record with the city. For homeowners in older pre-1994 properties, this permitting process also creates an official record of the upgrade — a document that carries real value when negotiating insurance rates or listing your home for sale.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Built for Melrose Park's HVHZ Demands
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two industry-leading impact door product lines — ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI — both of which are fully certified for Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone and specifically engineered to perform under the extreme conditions Melrose Park homeowners face. These aren't generic commercial products slapped into a residential frame. They are purpose-built, rigorously tested door systems that carry legitimate Florida Product Approval numbers and meet the large missile impact testing requirements of HVHZ compliance.

ESW impact doors are renowned for their robust frame construction, multi-point locking systems, and exceptional seal integrity. Whether you're choosing a single-panel impact entry door, a wide-format French door, or a sliding glass door for your back patio, ESW delivers clean sight lines and a finished aesthetic without compromising structural performance. Their doors are tested to exceed 170 mph wind loads and resist flying debris up to the large missile standard — meaning a 9-pound 2x4 traveling at 50 feet per second won't breach the door panel or frame. For Melrose Park homes where the front door or rear sliding door may be the most vulnerable point in the building envelope, that level of protection is non-negotiable.

CWI impact doors offer a complementary lineup with a strong emphasis on energy efficiency alongside hurricane protection. For homeowners in Melrose Park looking to reduce cooling costs during South Florida's punishing summers, CWI doors feature advanced glazing and thermal break technology that reduces heat transfer significantly compared to standard non-impact alternatives. Both product lines integrate multi-point locking hardware as a standard feature, which not only enhances hurricane resistance but also delivers meaningful improvements in home security — a real benefit in any residential neighborhood. Together, ESW and CWI give our customers a wide selection of styles, finishes, and configurations to suit any home's architecture and budget.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Doors Cost in Melrose Park — and Why the Investment Pays Off
section_cost.body:
For a home valued around $300,000 in Melrose Park, the cost of installing certified impact doors is a meaningful but highly strategic investment. Entry-level single impact door replacements typically start in the range of $1,500 to $3,000 installed, while sliding glass door replacements or French door configurations can run between $3,000 and $7,000 or more depending on size, glass type, and frame specifications. Homes with multiple exterior doors — a front entry, a back sliding door, and a side French door, for example — can expect a total project investment in the $6,000 to $15,000 range, depending on scope. These are real numbers, and we share them transparently because we believe informed homeowners make the best decisions.

What makes that investment compelling in Melrose Park is the multi-layered return it generates. First, impact doors directly reduce insurance premiums — often by hundreds of dollars per year through wind mitigation credits available under Florida law. Second, they add measurable resale value; buyers throughout Broward County actively seek HVHZ-compliant homes, and a fully permitted impact door upgrade is a documented, transferable asset. Third, impact doors reduce energy consumption by improving thermal performance, which adds up to real savings on monthly FPL bills through South Florida's long cooling season.

For homeowners who want to act now but prefer to spread payments over time, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down. There are no income minimums that would exclude lower-middle income households, and we work with multiple lending partners to find terms that work for your specific financial picture. Protecting a $300,000 home with properly rated doors should never be out of reach, and our financing options ensure it isn't.

section_insurance.h2: Unlock Significant Insurance Savings With HVHZ Impact Doors in Melrose Park
section_insurance.body:
Homeowners insurance in Broward County has been climbing for years, and Melrose Park residents in the 33312 ZIP code know the frustration of watching premiums rise regardless of their claims history. The good news is that installing certified impact doors is one of the most direct ways to take control of your insurance costs. Under Florida Statute 627.0629 and the accompanying wind mitigation credits program, homeowners who document hurricane-resistant opening protections — including HVHZ-rated impact doors — are entitled to meaningful discounts on the wind portion of their policy, which is typically the largest and most expensive component of a South Florida home insurance premium.

Major carriers operating in Broward County, including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, Heritage Insurance, and others, all recognize wind mitigation improvements when calculating premiums. After a Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installation, you'll receive documentation of your new doors' product approval numbers, wind speed ratings, and installation compliance — everything a licensed wind mitigation inspector needs to certify your home for premium credits. Many Melrose Park homeowners report annual savings of $300 to $800 or more after completing a full impact door upgrade, with larger savings possible when combined with impact window replacements.

Beyond the annual savings, impact doors also protect you from the catastrophic financial exposure of a storm claim gone wrong. If your non-impact door fails during a hurricane and interior water damage results, you may face a high deductible — in Florida, wind deductibles are often 2% to 5% of the insured value of the home, which on a $300,000 property means $6,000 to $15,000 out of pocket before insurance pays a dollar. Impact doors dramatically reduce the likelihood of that scenario ever occurring, acting as both a physical shield and a financial firewall against hurricane-related losses.

faqs.q: Do I really need HVHZ-rated impact doors in Melrose Park even though it's not on the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Melrose Park's location in Broward County places it within Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone regardless of its distance from the water. The HVHZ designation is based on regional wind speed data and building code jurisdiction, not proximity to the shoreline. With a 170 mph design wind speed and a history of damaging storms including Andrew, Wilma, and Irma, inland Melrose Park homes face the same code requirements and real-world risks as coastal properties. HVHZ certification is mandatory — not optional — for any impact door installed here.

faqs.q: How much will impact doors cost for my Melrose Park home, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Costs vary based on the number, size, and style of doors being replaced, but most Melrose Park homeowners can expect to invest between $1,500 and $7,000 per door opening installed. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down, making it manageable for households across all income levels. We provide free, no-obligation quotes so you can see exact pricing before committing. When you factor in insurance savings and increased home value, most customers find the investment pays for itself within a few years.

faqs.q: What permits are required for impact door replacement in Melrose Park, and who handles them?
faqs.a: Impact door replacements in Melrose Park require a permit through the City of Fort Lauderdale Building Division, which oversees building inspections for the area. Required documentation includes Florida Product Approval numbers for the specific door system being installed, a Notice of Commencement for larger projects, and a final inspection upon completion. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles all permitting paperwork on your behalf from submission through final sign-off, ensuring your installation is fully compliant with HVHZ and Florida Building Code requirements. You won't need to navigate city hall on your own.

faqs.q: How long does the impact door installation process take for a Melrose Park home?
faqs.a: For most Melrose Park homeowners, the timeline from contract signing to completed installation runs approximately three to six weeks. This includes the permitting process with the City of Fort Lauderdale Building Division, product lead time for your selected ESW or CWI doors, and the installation day itself. Most standard door replacements are completed in a single day by our in-house installation crew. We keep you informed at every stage and schedule around your availability. Homes with multiple door replacements may require an additional day depending on scope.

faqs.q: Will installing impact doors actually lower my homeowners insurance premium in Broward County?
faqs.a: Yes — in most cases, significantly. Florida law requires insurers to offer wind mitigation discounts to homeowners who install hurricane-resistant opening protections like HVHZ-rated impact doors. After your installation, a licensed wind mitigation inspector can document your upgrade, and you submit that report to your insurer to receive updated premium credits. Broward County homeowners commonly see annual savings of $300 to $800 or more depending on their carrier and coverage level. Over a 10-year period, those savings can offset a substantial portion of the original installation cost.

cta_body: Get your free, no-obligation quote from Premier Impact Windows & Roofing today — we'll handle every detail from permits to final inspection, so you can rest easy knowing your home is built to withstand whatever the next hurricane season brings.
